title: links.yaml config schema and parser
description: Implement the config layer: the links.yaml schema definition, a parser that reads and validates it, and a writer that updates it safely. This module is imported by all commands that read or modify the user's link page config.

features: Define the full links.yaml schema in src/config.js with these top-level fields: name (string, required), bio (string, optional), avatar (string path, optional), theme (string, default 'minimal-dark'), domain (string, optional), links (array of link objects)

Link object fields: label (string, required), url (string, required), icon (string emoji, optional), description (string, optional), scheduled_from (ISO date string, optional), scheduled_until (ISO date string, optional)
Implement readConfig(filePath) — reads and parses links.yaml using js-yaml, validates required fields, returns parsed object or throws descriptive error
Implement writeConfig(filePath, config) — serializes config back to YAML and writes atomically (write to temp file, rename)
Implement findConfig() — walks up from cwd to find the nearest links.yaml, throws if not found
Implement validateConfig(config) — validates schema, collects all errors, returns { valid: boolean, errors: string[] }
Implement validateUrl(url) — basic URL validation (must start with http:// or https://)
Export all functions as named exports from src/config.js
Add schema constant DEFAULT_CONFIG with sensible defaults for new projects

notes:

Atomic write pattern: write to links.yaml.tmp then fs.rename(). No external validation library — keep it lean. Throw errors with actionable messages like 'links.yaml: missing required field name'.
